diary:
Sunny Monday felt like it was waiting for me all morning. I woke up in Renton Highlands, made a slow breakfast, and fiddled with messages like they could change the day if I stared long enough. I picked a vinyl sleeve to take with me—just in case the record shop had the right reissue. The drive to Issaquah was easy, and the playlist kept my shoulders loose until I rolled into the courthouse with time to spare.

Work at Issaquah Courthouse moved steadily: translations, interpreting, the usual mix of careful wording and quiet urgency. Around lunch, I wandered out near Pike Place Market for an iced drink and let the sun hit my face for a minute too long. Capitol Hill’s Schoolkids Records smelled like fresh cardboard and dust—my favorite kind of browsing. Later, at Volunteer Park, I pretended I was out for “mushrooms,” though mostly I just enjoyed the greenery.

By evening, the USA vs Wales match pulled everyone downtown early. I ended up at Pike Brewing in Pioneer Square with a snack bag and a warm, buzzing kind of happiness—like the day had finally landed right.
thought_process: I planned my day around two things: keeping my work mornings calm and leaving enough flexibility for the city’s rhythm. With the light rail closed between Westlake and University District, I stayed comfortable with my car route and didn’t rely on transit for the midday stretch. I chose to lunch near Pike Place Market because it’s a quick reset between hearings—easy to step out without losing momentum. At the record shop, I asked about reissues because I’ve learned the clerk’s knowledge beats my own blind luck. The World Cup airing at 11am made downtown feel like it was shifting gears, so I headed early to catch pregame without rushing. My mood stayed steady through court work, then lifted noticeably once the sun and music-and-football energy overlapped.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
